content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Closed the two pending sync-safety amendments additively. In `src/cli/project.rs`, `claim_bead_in_br()` now only treats a recovered flush as proof of a prior successful claim when `sync_if_dirty()` explicitly reports `update_bead_status(bead_id, "in_progress")`; unrelated recovered work or a bead already showing `in_progress` no longer short-circuit the explicit `br update`, and the claim regression suite now covers that collision case plus the legacy-marker fallback path. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, `handle_propose_new_bead()` no longer health-gates the entire function up front; the beads-health guard now runs only immediately before mutation branches (`add_dependency`/`create_bead`), so read-only replay and reuse paths remain idempotent even when `.beads/issues.jsonl` is temporarily conflicted or missing. I also updated the existing malformed/conflict tests to assert the new read-only preflight behavior and added a regression proving journaled-bead reuse still succeeds without issuing a new `br` mutation under unhealthy bead-export state.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the existing recovered-claim retry path and `handle_propose_new_bead()` control flow to pinpoint where the remaining false-positive claim short-circuit and over-broad health gate were still applied.
2. [Completed] Patch `src/cli/project.rs` so recovered flushes only skip the explicit claim when `sync_if_dirty()` proves this exact bead/status update was replayed, and extend the claim regressions to cover unrelated recovered work plus the legacy-marker path.
3. [Completed] Patch `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs` so beads-health validation runs only on mutation branches, then add regression coverage showing read-only journaled-bead reuse still succeeds under conflicted bead-export state while mutation paths remain blocked.
4. [Completed] Run formatting and Rust validation gates on the finished tree, including focused regressions, the full test suite, and strict clippy.
5. [Intended] Run the repository's authoritative `nix build` gate on the final tree.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` exited 0.
- Focused regressions passed: `cargo test claim_bead_in_br -- --nocapture` and `cargo test handle_propose_new_bead -- --nocapture`, including the new `claim_bead_in_br_does_not_treat_other_operator_status_after_unrelated_flush_as_our_claim` and `handle_propose_new_bead_reuses_existing_journaled_bead_without_mutation_health_gate` cases.
- `cargo test` exited 0 on the full suite after a rerun, including 940 passing library tests with 1 ignored test, 241 passing CLI tests with 2 ignored tests, 1 passing `run_attach_tmux` test, 794 passing unit tests, and doc-tests with 9 ignored examples.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` exited 0.
- `nix build` was launched on the final tree, reached the release build for `/nix/store/lmmqyxycb298b8adw258yajy51bsgxl0-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, updated `result` to `/nix/store/hxvnnggamp7w50n4cvyfcdy00gkywq3z-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0`, and was still running `ralph-burning-stable-conformance` when interrupted after a prolonged no-output wait, so no final success/failure exit was observed.

## Outstanding Risks

- `nix build` did not reach a final exit in this session. It advanced into `/nix/store/lmmqyxycb298b8adw258yajy51bsgxl0-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, updated `result -> /nix/store/hxvnnggamp7w50n4cvyfcdy00gkywq3z-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0`, and was still running the release `ralph-burning-stable-conformance` harness before I interrupted it after a prolonged no-output wait, so the authoritative Nix gate remains unresolved.
- Pre-existing live orchestration-state edits under `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni44-1/` were left untouched and remain out of review scope per the task instructions.
